author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
hari he!
prapañce paḍiyā, agati hoiyā,
nā dekhi’ upāya āra
agatira gati, caraṇe śaraṇa,
tomāya karinu sāra

karama geyāna, kichu nāhi mora,
sādhana⋅bhajana nāi
tumi kṛpāmaya, āmi ta’ kāṅgāla,
ahaitukī kṛpā cāi

vākya⋅manovega, krodha⋅jihvā⋅vega,
udara⋅upastha⋅vega
miliyā e saba, saṁsāre bhāsāye,
diteche paramodvega

aneka jatane, se⋅saba damane,
chāḍiyāchi āśā āmi
anāthera nātha, ḍāki tava nāma,
ekhana bharasā tumi
translation_intro: From The Harmonist, March 1929. Edited by Bhaktisiddhanta Sarasvati.
en_translation:
Hari! Fallen into this world, Rendered helpless, I find no other way of deliverance, Guide of the guideless! The Refuge of Thy feet I seek as essential.

Capacity for work or Knowledge I have none; I neither serve Thee nor try. Thou art full of mercy, I am destitute, indeed; Thy causeless mercy I crave.

The activities of speach and mind, Anger, palate, The stomach and organ of generation,- All these joining together, Casting me adrift on the stream of the world, Are giving me great trouble.

The hope of subduing them, By using every care, I have given up. Lord of the lordless! I call upon Thy Name. Thou art now the Hope.
